data.name: Senior Product Manager
data.jobDescription:
Overview

Are you passionate about the breadth of Microsoft’s customers, from developers to administrators to end users? Do you want to help accelerate the developer ecosystem around Microsoft 365, the most popular productivity suite on the planet, and make Copilot an indispensable tool for every user of Excel?  

If so, the Excel Chat and Office Extensibility team would love to talk to you. 

About the Team 

Our team plays a key role in the Microsoft 365 developer story. We are responsible for the platform that allows developers (both first- and third-party) to integrate with and extend Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook across Windows, Mac, Office Online, iOS, and Android. We also partner closely with teams across Microsoft 365 to deliver a cohesive platform story for all Office developers. 

We do this by both fostering one of the world’s largest developer ecosystems around our Win32 apps, and building a modern extensibility model that’s built on web technologies and runs across platforms. Building a great platform isn’t just about APIs – we also think deeply about developer and user experience and ensure that from documentation to tooling to in-product UX to an in-app marketplace, we have everything our ecosystem needs to build powerful, deeply integrated solutions that enable customers to use our apps more efficiently and effectively. 

We are a diverse, worldwide, passionate, customer-obsessed team with a highly collaborative environment, focused on ensuring that we have a world-class platform and a successful, thriving partner ecosystem. We take pride in working as hard on team culture as we do on product, building an environment where we understand and respect each other and every person feels like they can bring their whole selves and do their best work. 

You would join the team as a Senior Product Manager working on the modern, web-based add-in model for Office, enabling developers to build deep integrations with Word, Excel, and PowerPoint that look and feel like Office, are easy for users to acquire/use/embrace, and travel with them across devices and platforms seamlessly. You’ll do this by building a platform that makes it easy for developers to build powerful, performant, highly-quality integrations using standard web technologies and tools. 

This position is an excellent opportunity for anyone who’s passionate about developer and customer success, as you need to maintain deep empathy for the Office user base, ensuring we build and maintain trust with both our ecosystem (allowing customers to leverage 3rd party integrations while staying current with updates to our products) and our users (ensuring that the experiences built on the platform are high-quality and look / behave like native features). 

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.



Responsibilities

We’re looking for someone who is ready to jump in and be a key contributor on the team, having immediate impact by: 

  • Deeply understanding the needs of Office developers (both first- and third-party).
  • Seeking out community input whenever possible, and using their feedback to guide your work.
  • Closely engaging with partners to help them succeed in building and maintaining integrations.
  • Partnering closely within our team and outside of it to deliver the platform.
  • Driving near-term optimization and delivery as well as the long-term strategy for supporting our ecosystem and helping it move forward.
  • Thinking big and moving quickly with a sharp focus on telemetry, experimentation, and business outcomes.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree AND 5+ years experience in product/service/program management or software development
    • OR equivalent experience. 
  • Are a passionate problem solver, who continuously strives to make things better.
  • Enjoy wearing multiple hats and digging in to make things happen. 
  • Are equally comfortable articulating the big picture and refining the small details of a design.
  • Are customer and data obsessed, always learning, and refining your features to deliver maximum business impact.
  • Love pushing for clarity in communication and can explain large, complex systems, in simple-straightforward language.
  • Have a strong drive to take a broad view on platform, thinking not only about the traditional topics of APIs, extension points, etc. but also about how extensions impact and integrate with the user experience.
  • Know how to balance passion and opinion with being a great team player. 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree AND 8+ years experience in product/service/program management or software development
    • OR equivalent experience.
  • 2+ years experience taking a product, feature, or experience to market (e.g., design, addressing product market fit, and launch, internal tool/framework).
  • 4+ years experience improving product metrics for a product, feature, or experience in a market (e.g., growing customer base, expanding customer usage, avoiding customer churn).
  • 4+ years experience disrupting a market for a product, feature, or experience (e.g., competitive disruption, taking the place of an established competing product).

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.



Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

blogs.title: Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely ru
blogs.description: As digital sovereignty becomes a strategic requirement, organizations are rethinking how they deploy critical infrastructure and AI capabilities under tighter regulatory expectations and higher risk conditions. Microsoft’s approach to sovereignty is grounded in enabling enterprises, public sectors and regulated industries to participate in the digital economy securely, independently and on their own terms. The Microsoft Sovereign Cloud brings together productivity, security and cloud workloads to span both... The post  Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely running even when completely disconnected   appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .

A key milestone on this journey was our aim to match 100% of our annual global electricity consumption...
